chunk_summary: The OSCE checklist for nutrition counselling assesses key skills in motivational interviewing, safety screening, goal setting, and interprofessional planning, ensuring structured and effective patient interactions.
learning_objectives: Understand and apply the OSCE checklist to assess nutrition counselling performance.
Demonstrate motivational interviewing techniques, including reflective statements and open-ended questions.
Develop SMART goals with barrier plans for patient adherence.
Identify red flags and determine when referrals are necessary.
key_facts: The OSCE checklist uses a done/partial/not done rating system for performance assessment.
Safety screening includes identifying red flags such as weight loss, dehydration, and restrictive eating danger signs.
Motivational interviewing requires at least one reflective statement, one affirmation, and one open-ended question.
The 5A’s structure (ASK, ADVISE, ASSESS, ASSIST, ARRANGE) must be completed with a timebound follow-up plan.
SMART goals should be measurable in the home environment and include a barrier plan.
Confidentiality and stigma safeguards are essential, especially for adolescents.
reference_quote: Motivational Interviewing emphasizes the use of reflective listening, affirmations, and open-ended questions to facilitate behavior change. (Source: Motivational Interviewing: Helping People Change, 3rd edition)
document_key_topics: SMART goal setting and follow-up planning
